assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Turning Points in American Church History: How Pivotal Events Shaped a Nation and a Faith, Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Chapter 7 The Benevolent Empire, p.131-151

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜ Identify three ideas that were new to you from this chapter and explain why those ideas captured your attention.

Chapter 1 The Church Fathers: Who Is Included?, p.1-4
and
Chapter 2 Clement and the Corinthian Coup, p. 5-10

➜ NARRATE
Explain how developing a literary relationship with the early Church Fathers can impact a person's spiritual life.

⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch. 3 The Didache: Out of the Mist of History, p.11-17

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜ WRITE
If you were writing a catechism, what three areas of instruction would you prioritize first and why?

➜ DEFINE & REFLECT

Ch.7 Justin and the Philosopher's Cloak, p.47-57

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜ EXPLAIN
Explain why Justin considered himself a philosopher. Why did Justin refer to Christ as a "key that unlocked all doors..."

⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.8 Irenaeus and the Gnostic Threat, p.58-67

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜ LIST
In your journal, identify the heresies that Irenaeus confronted, and make a list of the potential heresies the church is battling against today. Are there similarities between today's heresies and those that Irenaeus confronted?

⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

Ch.9 Irenaeus and the Battle for the Bible, p.68-76

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜EXPLAIN
Explain how the canon of Scripture was established to a friend or family member.

⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.10 Clement and the School of Alexandria, p.77-87

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜ CONTRAST
Contrast gnosticism with Clement's understand of a "true" gnostic.

⍞ Materials: When The Church Was Young: Voices of the Early Fathers, a Journal or Notebook

➜ READ
Ch.11 Origen: Zeal and Genius, p.88-101

➜ NARRATE
Take a moment and either silently or aloud summarize the main ideas of this section.

➜ DESCRIBE
In your journal, describe the catechetical institute of Alexandria and include the academic disciplines that were studied. Why did the institute include disciplines beyond biblical studies?

Read the following passage and explain what the author is communicating:
"What they passed on was not primarily a book, or a set of truths. It was a life. It was the truth...There are some things that you can only learn by absorbing them. You absorb such a thing by living with people who have it. And once absorbed, you can never fully articulate it. But you can live it, and you can pass it on to those you live with."
